channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Hegseth meets with moderate Sen. Collins as he lobbies for key votes in the Senate
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee to lead the Defense Department said he had a “wonderful conversation” with Maine Sen.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick And Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: GOP attorneys general offer support for Trump FBI pick Kash Patel, urge senators to do the same
item.description: Dozens of Republican state attorneys general are voicing their support for Kash Patel, President-elect Trump's choice to become director of the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic senators urge Biden to act on temporary protections for migrants
item.description: Democratic senators are urging President Joe Biden to extend temporary protections for migrants in the U.S. before he leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Colleen Long And Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden says healthy women help US prosperity as he highlights White House initiative on their health
item.description: President Joe Biden says having healthy women is as important for the U.S. economy as it is for the women themselves.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Secretary of State Blinken is returning to the Mideast in his latest diplomatic foray
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken is returning to the Middle East this week on his 12th visit since the Israel-Hamas war erupted last year but his first since the ouster of Syrian President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI Director Wray says he intends to resign at the end of Biden's term in January
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray says he plans to resign at the end of President Joe Biden’s term in January.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Manchin, Sinema tank Schumer lame-duck effort to secure Dem majority on top labor board
item.description: Republicans called foul on Sen. Chuck Schumer, D-N.Y., and Democrats for trying to push through a nominee that would have kept the National Labor Relations Board Democratic-controlled, preventing President-elect Trump from naming the new chair.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US inflation ticked up last month as some price pressures remain persistent
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Fueled by pricier used cars, hotel rooms and groceries, inflation in the United States moved slightly higher last month in the latest sign that some price pressures remain elevated.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kimberly Guilfoyle gets Greece as consolation prize after Donald Trump Jr. dumps her: Source
item.description: Kimberly Guilfoyle has been tapped to be the U.S. Ambassador to Greece in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's withdrawn nominees latest in a long line of Cabinet members who never were
item.description: Many presidents face failed Cabinet picks so when former Rep. Matt Gaetz withdrew from consideration for President-elect Donald Trump's Cabinet, it wasn't unprecedented, though Trump's style has been.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Donald Trump is returning to the world stage. So is his trolling
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump’s recent dinner with Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau and his visit to Paris for the reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral were not just exercises in policy and diplomacy. They were also prime trolling opportunities for Trump.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Making a $1B investment in the US? Trump pledges expedited permits — but there are hurdles
item.description: Much like other Trump plans, the idea is likely to run into regulatory and legislative hurdles, including a landmark law that requires federal agencies to consider the environmental impact before deciding on major projects.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: House advances annual defense policy bill, teeing up final vote
item.description: The House advanced the annual defense policy bill on Tuesday, teeing up the mammoth measure for a final vote later this week. The chamber voted 211-207 along party lines to adopt the rule — which governs debate for legislation — for the sprawling bill, formally opening up debate in the chamber.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell receives medical attention after falling
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) received medical attention after falling following a Republican lunch meeting Tuesday. A spokesman for McConnell said the GOP leader tripped after the lunch. "He sustained a minor cut to the face and sprained his wrist.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden to tout economic legacy in Tuesday speech
item.description: President Biden will deliver remarks Tuesday afternoon touting his economic agenda and making the case that it has been more effective than the kinds of tax cuts likely to be implemented by the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Here's how the US is countering the Islamic State group during Syria's upheaval
item.description: The U.S. has had troops in Syria for the last decade to battle IS. The tumult following a rebel offensive that toppled Assad has raised fears of an Islamic State resurgence.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans pick Mast to lead House Foreign Affairs panel
item.description: House Republicans selected Rep. Brian Mast (R-Fla.) to chair the House Foreign Affairs Committee in the 119th Congress, sources told The Hill, as the conference works to organize its panels for the next two years of Republican-controlled Washington Republicans also selected Rep.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Manchin, Welch unveil bill placing term limits on Supreme Court
item.description: West Virginia Sen. Joe Manchin (I) and Vermont Sen. Peter Welch (D) have introduced a resolution to impose 18-year term limits on Supreme Court justices, which would require some turnover on the high court every two years.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump focuses on tariffs, not retribution, in first network TV interview
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump lays out plan for his first 100 days during interview with NBC News' "Meet the Press.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: NYC mayor will not completely rule out switch to Republican Party
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New York Democratic Mayor Eric Adams sent shockwaves through the political scene Friday morning after refusing to rule out running for another term as a Republican.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams on City of Yes plan passing City Council vote
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City Council has approved Mayor Eric Adams' ambitious City of Yes plan. The $5 billion plan will rezone the Big Apple for the first time in six decades. It's estimated to create more than 80,000 new housing units over 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: NYC plan would abolish income tax for certain residents: See if you qualify
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new proposal would abolish New York City’s personal income tax for nearly half a million qualifying residents.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11
